Package renderer.algebra

Class Matrix

java.lang.Object
renderer.algebra.ArrayBase
renderer.algebra.Matrix
public class Matrix extends ArrayBase

    Creates an identity matrix of the given size with name "I"+size (e.g., "I3" for a 3x3 identity matrix).

    Creates an identity matrix of the given size and name.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_NAME

      public static final String DEFAULT_NAME
      The default name of the matrix.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • Matrix

      public Matrix(String name, int numRows, int numCols) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a named Matrix of size nRows x nCols.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the matrix
      numRows - number of rows
      numCols - number of columns
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the matrix dimensions are invalid

    • Matrix

      public Matrix(int nRows, int nCols) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a Matrix of size nRows x nCols.
      Parameters:
      nRows - number of rows
      nCols - number of columns
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the matrix dimensions are invalid

  • Method Details

    • createIdentity

      public static final Matrix createIdentity(int size) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates an identity matrix of the given size with name "I"+size (e.g., "I3" for a 3x3 identity matrix).
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the identity matrix (number of rows and columns)
      Returns:
      a new identity matrix of size size x size
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the matrix dimensions are invalid

    • createIdentity

      public static final Matrix createIdentity(String name, int size) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates an identity matrix of the given size and name.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the matrix
      size - the size of the identity matrix (number of rows and columns)
      Returns:
      a new identity matrix of size size x size with the given name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the matrix dimensions are invalid

    • createRandom

      public static Matrix createRandom(String name, int nRows, int nCols) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a random matrix of size nRows x nCols. Each element is filled with a random value between 0.0 (inclusive) and 1.0 (exclusive).
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the matrix
      nRows - the number of rows (must be strictly positive)
      nCols - the number of columns (must be strictly positive)
      Returns:
      a new nRows x nCols matrix filled with random values
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the matrix dimensions are invalid

    • getSubMatrix

      public Matrix getSubMatrix(int offsetRow, int offsetCol, int numRows, int numCols) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Extracts a submatrix of size numRows x numCols with top-left corner at position (offsetRow, offsetCol). This method does NOT modify the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      offsetRow - the starting row index (0-based)
      offsetCol - the starting column index (0-based)
      numRows - the number of rows to extract (must be at least 1)
      numCols - the number of columns to extract (must be at least 1)
      Returns:
      a new Matrix containing the extracted numRows x numCols submatrix
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the submatrix dimensions are invalid or extend beyond the matrix bounds

    • transpose

      public final Matrix transpose()
      Get the transposed matrix. This method does NOT modify the current matrix.
      Returns:
      a new Matrix that is the transpose of this matrix

    • multiply

      public final Matrix multiply(Matrix m) throws SizeMismatchException
      Matrix/Matrix multiplication. This method does NOT modify the current matrix or the operand.
      Parameters:
      m - the matrix to multiply with
      Returns:
      a new Matrix containing the result of this * m
      Throws:
      SizeMismatchException - if the matrix sizes do not match for multiplication

    • multiply

      public final Vector multiply(Vector v) throws SizeMismatchException
      Matrix/Vector multiplication. This method does NOT modify the current matrix or the operand.
      Parameters:
      v - the vector to multiply with
      Returns:
      a new Vector containing the result of this * v
      Throws:
      SizeMismatchException - if the matrix sizes do not match for multiplication

    • add

      public final Matrix add(Matrix m) throws SizeMismatchException
      Matrix/Matrix addition. This method does NOT modify the current matrix or the operand.
      Parameters:
      m - the matrix to add with
      Returns:
      a new Matrix containing the result of this + m
      Throws:
      SizeMismatchException - if the matrix sizes do not match for addition

    • subtract

      public final Matrix subtract(Matrix m) throws SizeMismatchException
      Matrix/Matrix subtraction : this - m. This method does NOT modify the current matrix or the operand.
      Parameters:
      m - the matrix to subtract
      Returns:
      a new Matrix containing the result of this - m
      Throws:
      SizeMismatchException - if the matrix sizes do not match for subtraction

    • set

      public void set(int i, int j, double value)
      Sets the element on row i and column j to the given value. This method MODIFIES the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      i - the row index
      j - the column index
      value - the value to set

    • setCol

      public void setCol(int i, Vector v)
      Sets the i-th column in the matrix to the given vector. This method MODIFIES the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      i - the column index (0-based)
      v - the vector to set (its size must match the number of rows)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the vector size does not match the number of rows or if the column index is invalid

    • getCol

      public final Vector getCol(int i)
      Get the elements of the i-th column of the matrix as a vector. This method does NOT modify the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      i - the column index
      Returns:
      a new Vector containing the elements of column i
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the column index is invalid

    • setRow

      public void setRow(int i, Vector v)
      Sets the i-th row in the matrix to the given vector. This method MODIFIES the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      i - the row index (0-based)
      v - the vector to set (its size must match the number of columns)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the vector size does not match the number of columns or if the row index is invalid

    • getRow

      public final Vector getRow(int i)
      Get a copy of the elements of the i-th row of the matrix as a vector. This method does NOT modify the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      i - the row index
      Returns:
      a new Vector containing the elements of row i
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the row index is invalid

    • get

      public final double get(int i, int j)
      Gets the element at the specified position.
      Parameters:
      i - the row index (0-based)
      j - the column index (0-based)
      Returns:
      the element at position (i, j)

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a Matlab compatible representation of the Matrix.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      the string representation of the matrix

    • getNRows

      public final int getNRows()
      Returns the number of rows.
      Returns:
      the number of rows

    • getNCols

      public final int getNCols()
      Returns the number of columns.
      Returns:
      the number of columns

    • getDimensionString

      public String getDimensionString()
      Description copied from class: ArrayBase
      Gets a string representation of the dimensions of this object. Subclasses should override to provide meaningful dimension info.
      Overrides:
      getDimensionString in class ArrayBase
      Returns:
      a string describing the dimensions

    • scale

      public Matrix scale(double f)
      Multiplies the Matrix by the given constant. This method does NOT modify the current matrix.
      Parameters:
      f - the constant to multiply the matrix by
      Returns:
      a new Matrix containing the result of this * f
